meta.source_paper: PSE encrypted_backup_recovery research (2026), SLIP-39, Juels-Ristenpart 2014 (Honey Encryption)

risk_domain.name: Backup Encryption and Recovery Security
risk_domain.description: Detect weak backup encryption, stale guardians, quantum-vulnerable backup key exchange, lack of coercion resistance, and non-deterministic secrets that cannot be recovered from seed. Protects the recoverability and confidentiality of wallet secrets.

capabilities: full on-chain observation of all encrypted backup data (permanent, public)
offline brute-force against password-encrypted backups (GPU/ASIC acceleration)
future CRQC access for ECDH-encrypted backup key exchange
coercion of user to reveal backup passwords under duress
limitations: no physical access to hardware wallets or secure enclaves
cannot compromise a threshold of independent guardians simultaneously (assumed)

H1_password_only_backup.name: Password-only backup encryption
H1_password_only_backup.description: Backup encrypted solely with a user-chosen password and no additional authentication factor. Vulnerable to offline brute-force: even with Argon2id KDF, a weak password (6-8 chars) is crackable with GPU clusters. On-chain backups are permanently available for offline attack with no rate-limiting.

signals.name: single_factor_encryption
signals.description: Backup ciphertext uses password-derived key (Argon2/scrypt/PBKDF2) with no threshold shares or hardware key

signals.name: weak_kdf_parameters
signals.description: KDF parameters below recommended minimums (Argon2id: <64MB memory, <3 iterations; scrypt: <N=2^20)

signals.name: no_hardware_binding
signals.description: Backup encryption has no hardware key component (e.g., YubiKey HMAC challenge-response)

detection.threshold: single_factor_encryption AND weak_kdf_parameters triggers BLOCK for new backups, WARNING for existing

recommendations.description: Split backup key into 2-of-3 Shamir shares: password-derived share + guardian share + hardware key share
Use Argon2id with >=256MB memory, >=4 iterations, 32-byte salt. This raises brute-force cost but does not eliminate single-factor risk.

H1_password_only_backup.fundamental_limitation: Password entropy is bounded by human memory. No KDF can make a 6-character password secure against a determined attacker with GPU clusters and permanent access to the ciphertext.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Generate N backup ciphertexts with varying password strengths and KDF parameters
benchmark_scenario.metric: estimated brute-force time per backup given attacker budget (e.g., $10K GPU rental)
benchmark_scenario.baseline: 8-char random password with Argon2id (64MB, 3 iter): ~months on GPU cluster; 6-char: ~hours

H2_stale_guardians.name: Stale guardians
H2_stale_guardians.description: Social recovery guardian has not confirmed liveness in more than 6 months. Stale guardians may have lost their keys, changed addresses, or become unreachable. If enough guardians go stale, recovery threshold becomes unrecoverable. Adversary can also compromise stale guardians without detection.

signals.name: guardian_inactive
signals.description: Guardian address has no on-chain activity (transactions sent) in the last 6 months

signals.name: guardian_liveness_expired
signals.description: Guardian has not responded to a liveness check (on-chain heartbeat or signed attestation) within the configured period

signals.name: threshold_at_risk
signals.description: Number of active guardians minus stale guardians is below or equal to the recovery threshold

detection.threshold: threshold_at_risk triggers BLOCK, guardian_inactive triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Send liveness check request to all guardians. Replace any who do not respond within 30 days.
Increase guardian set size above threshold to tolerate stale members (e.g., 3-of-5 instead of 2-of-3)

H2_stale_guardians.fundamental_limitation: Guardian liveness is fundamentally probabilistic. On-chain activity is a proxy, not proof of guardian readiness. A guardian can be active on-chain but have lost their recovery key.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Sample N social recovery wallets, check guardian activity timestamps
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of recovery setups where stale guardians bring effective threshold below recoverability
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Argent reported ~15% of guardians become unreachable within 1 year

H3_quantum_vulnerable_backup_encryption.name: Quantum-vulnerable backup encryption
H3_quantum_vulnerable_backup_encryption.description: Backup encrypted using ECDH-derived key exchange (e.g., recipient public key + ephemeral ECDH) and stored on-chain permanently. A future CRQC breaks ECDH, recovers the encryption key, and decrypts the backup, exposing all wallet secrets including seed phrases, viewing keys, and nullifier keys.

signals.name: ecdh_key_in_backup_header
signals.description: Backup ciphertext header contains an ephemeral secp256k1 public key indicating ECDH key exchange

signals.name: backup_to_known_ecdh_contract
signals.description: Backup stored via contract known to use ECDH-only encryption (e.g., Tornado Cash encrypted notes)

signals.name: no_symmetric_only_flag
signals.description: Backup encryption metadata does not indicate symmetric-only encryption (AES-256 with password KDF)

detection.threshold: backup_to_known_ecdh_contract triggers BLOCK, ecdh_key_in_backup_header triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Encrypt backup with AES-256-GCM using password-derived key (Argon2id). AES-256 is PQ-safe; the password is the anchor, not ECDH.
If threshold/guardian recovery requires public-key encryption, use hybrid ECDH + ML-KEM-768 key exchange

H3_quantum_vulnerable_backup_encryption.fundamental_limitation: Backups already on-chain with ECDH-only encryption cannot be retroactively protected. The ciphertext is permanent. Only new backups can use PQ-safe schemes.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Scan on-chain backup contracts for ECDH ephemeral key presence in backup ciphertexts
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of on-chain backups using ECDH-only vs symmetric-only vs hybrid encryption
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Tornado Cash used ECDH-encrypted notes; most on-chain backup schemes use ECDH

H4_no_coercion_resistance.name: No coercion resistance
H4_no_coercion_resistance.description: Backup/recovery system has a single password that reveals the real seed with no deniable encryption layer. Under duress, the user cannot present a plausible decoy seed while keeping the real one hidden. This matters for dissidents, journalists, and anyone crossing hostile borders with crypto assets.

signals.name: single_decryption_path
signals.description: Backup ciphertext has exactly one valid decryption key -- no hidden volume or deniable layer

signals.name: deterministic_seed_output
signals.description: Any valid password deterministically produces the same seed -- attacker can verify they have the real one

signals.name: no_honey_encryption
signals.description: Wrong passwords produce decryption errors instead of plausible fake seeds (no Honey Encryption)

detection.threshold: single_decryption_path AND no_honey_encryption triggers WARNING for high-risk user profiles

recommendations.description: Implement False-Bottom Encryption (Ahmad et al. 2023): same ciphertext, two passwords, two plaintexts. Real seed under real password, decoy seed under coercion password.
recommendations.user_cost: medium (must maintain two seeds, decoy must be plausible)

recommendations.description: Use Honey Encryption (Juels-Ristenpart 2014) so every wrong password produces a plausible-looking seed

H4_no_coercion_resistance.fundamental_limitation: Coercion resistance has a fundamental paradox: the attacker cannot prove all keys have been disclosed, so they may keep pressuring indefinitely. Deniable encryption provides plausible deniability, not guaranteed safety.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Generate backup ciphertexts with and without deniable encryption. Simulate coercion scenario.
benchmark_scenario.metric: probability that an informed attacker can distinguish real seed from decoy seed
benchmark_scenario.baseline: without deniable encryption: 100% (single password = single seed)

H5_nondeterministic_secrets_not_backed_u.name: Non-deterministic secrets not backed up
H5_nondeterministic_secrets_not_backed_u.description: Protocol generates random per-action secrets (e.g., original Tornado Cash deposit notes) that cannot be derived from the wallet seed. If the device is lost and only the seed is recovered, these secrets are permanently lost, making the associated funds or state irrecoverable.

signals.name: random_note_generation
signals.description: Transaction interacts with a protocol known to generate non-deterministic per-action secrets (e.g., Tornado Cash note = random nullifier + random secret)

signals.name: no_onchain_backup_for_note
signals.description: Non-deterministic secret was generated but no corresponding encrypted backup transaction was submitted on-chain

signals.name: seed_only_recovery_claimed
signals.description: Wallet claims seed-only recovery but uses protocols with non-deterministic state

recommendations.description: Immediately create an encrypted on-chain or off-chain backup of the non-deterministic secret after each protocol interaction
recommendations.user_cost: low (additional gas for on-chain backup, or local encrypted file)

recommendations.description: Use protocols that derive all secrets from seed (modern Railgun, Zcash Sapling+) instead of ones with random per-action secrets

H5_nondeterministic_secrets_not_backed_u.fundamental_limitation: Non-deterministic secrets are inherently unrecoverable from seed. The only options are explicit backup or protocol redesign. Cannot be fixed retroactively for secrets already generated without backup.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Enumerate wallet interactions with non-deterministic protocols, check for corresponding backup transactions
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of non-deterministic secrets with verified on-chain or off-chain backup
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Tornado Cash: 5-35% of deposits had on-chain encrypted backup (linkability concerns discouraged backup)

guardian_monitor.description: Monitor guardian addresses for on-chain activity, send liveness check requests, alert when guardians go stale or recovery threshold is at risk

backup_auditor.description: Analyze backup ciphertext metadata to detect encryption scheme, KDF parameters, key exchange method, and presence of deniable encryption layer. Recommend upgrades.

key_rotation_scheduler.description: Schedule and track key rotation events for guardian sets, backup encryption keys, and recovery configurations. Proactive share refresh support.

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring backup security posture across all recovery dimensions

methodology.step_1: Inventory all backup and recovery configurations (password, guardians, hardware keys, on-chain backups)
methodology.step_2: For each backup, assess: encryption strength, KDF parameters, PQ safety, deniability, guardian liveness
methodology.step_3: For non-deterministic protocol interactions, verify backup existence
methodology.step_4: Score overall backup security as composite of brute-force resistance, recoverability, and coercion resistance

combined_benchmark.target: All backups: PQ-safe encryption, multi-factor, liveness-checked guardians, deterministic or explicitly backed up secrets

adversary_levels.name: opportunistic
adversary_levels.description: Brute-force password-only backups with commodity hardware

adversary_levels.name: targeted
adversary_levels.description: Target specific user: brute-force + social-engineer guardians + coercion

adversary_levels.name: quantum_capable
adversary_levels.description: All attack vectors including CRQC against ECDH-encrypted backups

templates.summary: Backup security scan: {n_txs} configurations analyzed. {n_risky} at risk ({pct_risky}%). Guardian liveness: {guardian_liveness}%. Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.summary_clean: All {n_txs} backup configurations pass security checks. Guardians active, encryption PQ-safe.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}, I can {skill_action}. This improves backup security by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
templates.guardian_alert: Guardian {guardian_address} has been inactive for {inactive_days} days. Recovery threshold at risk: {active_guardians}/{total_guardians} active vs {threshold} required.
templates.backup_warning: WARNING: This backup uses {encryption_scheme} which is {vulnerability}. Recommended: {recommendation}.
